headline: Life under Israeli occupation inside Lebanon's 'yellow line'
description: In one of the few villages still inhabited within the 'yellow line,' a buffer zone occupied by the Israeli army, disappearances and restrictions on movement are alarming residents and making their lives increasingly difficult.

headline: War in Ukraine: Russia fired Oreshnik missile on city  occupied by its own forces
Ever-extreme Eric Zemmour prepares second bid for French president
How French courts became Europe's toughest on women returning from IS
description: Of the 23 terrorism trials scheduled in French courts in 2026, 18 involve women who joined the Islamic State organization in Syria. While their role was once overlooked, they are now being judged on equal footing with men.

headline: Bold new proposal calls for merging Paris with suburbs
description: Clément Beaune, the French high commissioner for strategy and planning, proposed a sweeping overhaul of the Greater Paris area and its local governments.

headline: Artist Louise Vo Tan brings the Louvre's stolen crown jewels back to life
description: 'They were dazzling, they looked like a meringue,' said Vo Tan, who filmed the museum's treasures before they were stolen and is now displaying them in hologram form in the building once used for the Port-Coton foghorn.

headline: Marjane Satrapi, powerful and outspoken artist who swore to 'stay true to herself'
description: Famous for her four-part graphic novel 'Persepolis' recounting her childhood in Iran during the Islamic Revolution, which she also adapted for the screen, Satrapi was also later involved with the Woman, Life, Freedom movement.
byline: Jacques Mandelbaum,  Frédéric Potet and  Roxana Azimi

The health of French presidents, a state secret
HSBC charged for helping former Lebanese central bank head embezzle hundreds of millions
description: The Swiss subsidiary of HSBC is suspected of having helped former Lebanese central bank chief Riad Salameh embezzle more than $300 million from the country's central bank. The charges mark a significant development in the French judicial investigation.

headline: Who is rich in France?
description: Is a childless couple wealthy if they earn €6,438 per month after taxes? A new report seeks to address the lack of set criteria for measuring this demographic.

headline: Thomas Piketty's lab proposes ambitious plan to cut global inequality and curb climate change
description: Building a more equitable world while respecting planetary limits is possible, economists from the World Inequality Lab assert in a report published Thursday. To achieve this, they say, targeted reductions in consumption in specific sectors, a drastic cut in fossil fuel use and changes in dietary habits will be necessary.

headline: Holding trials next to a prison threatens defendants' rights
French presidential election: 'Which of Attal or Glucksmann can form the broadest coalition'
